content: Just as the linguistic meaning of 'reflexion' encodes both physical light reflection and deliberate cognitive reflection in human language, LLM self-reflection mechanisms can be improved by explicitly splitting their memory modules into a 'passive reflection' layer that mirrors inconsistent solving perspectives and an 'active deliberative reflection' layer that curates these perspectives to update long-term agent memory, building on the dual semantic roots of the original term. This dual-layer design directly leverages the inherent duality of the concept 'reflexion' to address current limitations of single-perspective LLM self-reflection.
